schemaVersion: Ehel Grade 2 English Course Final Quiz v1.1
title: Grade 2 English Final Course Quiz
description: Complete three short sections to show what you can understand and use after Units 1-10.
subject: English
attemptsAllowed: Unlimited with targeted review between attempts
feedbackMode: Immediate child-friendly feedback with final mastery report

sections.title: Words and reading
sections.description: Show what words mean and use reading clues from across Grade 2.

sections.title: Communication and writing
sections.description: Apply English to speaking, directions, feedback and writing.

questions.question: Which day comes after Wednesday?
questions.options: Tuesday | Thursday | Friday | Sunday
questions.explanation: Thursday comes directly after Wednesday in the weekly calendar.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Who helps people who are sick?
questions.options: A doctor | A driver | A builder | A farmer
questions.explanation: A doctor examines and helps people who are ill.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which body part joins your shoulder to your hand?
questions.options: Arm | Toe | Neck | Knee
questions.explanation: Your arm extends from your shoulder to your hand.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which word means the brightness that lets us see things?
questions.options: Light | Shadow | Evening | Cloud
questions.explanation: Light is the brightness that makes objects visible.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which number word means 70?
questions.options: Seventeen | Seventy | Sixty | Seven
questions.explanation: Seventy is the number 70.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which animal is a small jumping insect that makes a chirping sound?
questions.options: Cricket | Butterfly | Worm | Ant
questions.explanation: A cricket jumps and is known for its chirping sound.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which plant parts grow underground and take in water?
questions.options: Roots | Flowers | Leaves | Fruit
questions.explanation: Roots anchor a plant and absorb water from the soil.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: What is a set of rooms inside a larger building where people live?
questions.options: An apartment | A hive | A cave | A hut
questions.explanation: An apartment is a home made of rooms within a larger building.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Where can you borrow books to read?
questions.options: A library | A station | A market | A hospital
questions.explanation: A library keeps books that people can read or borrow.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Why did Samira correct and improve her portfolio work?
questions.options: To show what she had learned | To make every page shorter | To remove all the pictures | To copy a classmate's work
questions.correctAnswer: To show what she had learned
questions.explanation: The capstone portfolio should demonstrate the learner's own growth and strongest English skills.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: This is Samira. ___ likes reading.
questions.options: She | He | They | We
questions.explanation: Use she for one girl or woman.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Adam ___ football.
questions.options: likes | like | liking | are like
questions.explanation: Use likes with one person named Adam.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: The firefighters ___ climbing the ladder.
questions.options: are | is | am | was
questions.explanation: Use are with the plural subject firefighters.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which is the correct -ing form of drive?
questions.options: driving | driveing | drvng | drives
questions.explanation: Drop the final e before adding -ing: drive becomes driving.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which sentence describes an action happening now?
questions.options: Selma is hopping. | Selma hopped yesterday. | Selma will hop. | Selma likes games.
questions.explanation: Is plus a verb ending in -ing shows an action happening now.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: There ___ one bright star above the house.
questions.options: is | are | am | were
questions.explanation: Use there is for one thing.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which sentence gives a correct comparison?
questions.options: A metre is longer than a centimetre. | A metre is long than a centimetre. | A metre longer a centimetre. | A metre is more long.
questions.correctAnswer: A metre is longer than a centimetre.
questions.explanation: Longer than is the correct comparison form.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Butterflies have colourful ___.
questions.options: wings | winges | wing | winge
questions.explanation: The plural noun wings completes the sentence accurately.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which question asks about a person?
questions.options: Who helped you? | Where is the bus? | What is your name? | How did you travel?
questions.explanation: Who is the question word used for a person.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which sentence states a future goal?
questions.options: I will read longer stories. | I read a story yesterday. | I am reading now. | I wrote a story.
questions.explanation: Will expresses a future action or goal.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which is a complete question about colour?
questions.options: What colour do you like? | Colour you? | Like blue. | What like?
questions.explanation: A complete question uses a question word, subject, verb and question mark.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which is the most polite way to ask about someone's job?
questions.options: What do you do? | Tell job now. | You job? | Give me work.
questions.explanation: What do you do? is a complete and polite question about work.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which instruction helps a friend move safely?
questions.options: Walk carefully and look ahead. | Run with your eyes closed. | Push past everyone. | Jump from a high table.
questions.explanation: A clear safety instruction uses an action that prevents harm.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which sentence is a clear sky observation?
questions.options: The orange sun is low in the evening sky. | Sky nice. | Orange there. | The sun sky low orange it.
questions.correctAnswer: The orange sun is low in the evening sky.
questions.explanation: The sentence gives a complete, ordered observation with useful describing words.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which instruction explains how to measure a table?
questions.options: Place the ruler at one edge and count the centimetres. | Guess and write any number. | Hold the ruler in the air. | Count the table legs only.
questions.correctAnswer: Place the ruler at one edge and count the centimetres.
questions.explanation: Accurate measurement begins at an edge and uses the marked units.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which sentence gives a complete insect fact?
questions.options: A butterfly has six legs and colourful wings. | Butterfly colourful. | Six and wings. | Has legs the butterfly.
questions.correctAnswer: A butterfly has six legs and colourful wings.
questions.explanation: The sentence has a subject, accurate details and end punctuation.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which sentence gives a helpful environmental instruction?
questions.options: Put litter in the bin, please. | Drop it beside the road. | Leave the tap running. | Pull up every plant.
questions.correctAnswer: Put litter in the bin, please.
questions.explanation: The instruction is polite and describes an action that protects the environment.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which sentence clearly describes a room's location?
questions.options: The bedroom is beside the bathroom. | Bedroom bathroom. | Beside is room. | The bedroom beside.
questions.correctAnswer: The bedroom is beside the bathroom.
questions.explanation: The complete sentence uses beside to explain location.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which direction is clearest?
questions.options: Walk past the library, then turn left at the market. | Go there somehow. | Left and maybe past. | The city is busy.
questions.correctAnswer: Walk past the library, then turn left at the market.
questions.explanation: Clear directions use ordered action words and recognizable landmarks.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

questions.question: Which feedback will help a learner improve a final project?
questions.options: Your ideas are clear; add punctuation to the last sentence. | It is bad. | Change everything. | I do not like it.
questions.correctAnswer: Your ideas are clear; add punctuation to the last sentence.
questions.explanation: Helpful feedback is kind, specific and gives a manageable next step.
questions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
questions.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 - All Grade 2 Units

reporting.learner: Total score, mastery status, section scores and recommended Unit review links
reporting.teacher: Latest attempt, attempt history, curriculum-area scores and source-unit scores

origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 cumulative assessment
